items.title: Two TrueNAS servers, one rack — the CORE-and-SCALE split I never designed
items.description:
Two TrueNAS servers. Same rack. One's on CORE, one's on SCALE.

If someone asked me why, the honest answer isn't a design decision. It's a series of arrivals — one workload trigger at a time, each landing on whichever server had the shape to accept it, until the current arrangement crystall

items.title: Building an AI Recruitment SaaS: Lessons Learned
items.description:
Five months ago I deleted two months of work and started over.

This is the honest version of what happened next building Beonmap, an AI-powered job discovery platform, as a bootstrapped team. The good parts, the expensive mistakes, and the problem we still haven't solved.<

items.title: Getting Real Numbers Into the VPA Model: The Commands and Tools
items.description: The companion post to this one, "What the VPA Recommender Is Actually Computing," walks through the decay-weighted percentile math, the OOM bump, and the calculator that lets you punch in numbers and see what VPA would recommend. All of that is only useful once you have real numbers to punch in.

items.title: translateZ()
items.description: The translateZ() function moves an element closer to or farther from the user. translateZ() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.
